headline: SOLID Principles
description: SOLID (Robert C. Martin, 2000s) is five principles for object-oriented design: S (Single Responsibility), O (Open/Closed), L (Liskov Substitution), I (Interface Segregation), D (Dependency Inversion). SOLID makes code more maintainable, testable, and flexible.

citation.name: Clean Architecture: A Craftsman's Guide (2025 Updated)
citation.author: Martin R.C.

citation.name: SOLID Principles in the Age of AI-Assisted Development (2025)
citation.author: multiple
